blockingIssues.description: player_sync_metadata writes in app/api/match-performance/route.ts record latest_riot_match_id/latest_riot_match_created_at from whichever match is currently analyzed, so backfilled stale or missing rows can incorrectly become the durable Riot-head metadata.

app/api/player-matches/stale-ids/route.ts uses a single player-level derivation_version, so refreshing one stale row can prevent other stale rows for that player from being detected even if they were never recomputed.
components/dashboard/lol-stats-dashboard.tsx only reloads stored matches when analyzedCount > 0, so stale-row-only refreshes are not surfaced in the DB-first view after reconciliation.
components/dashboard/lol-stats-dashboard.tsx hardcodes the recent reconciliation window at the call site instead of wiring a tracked bounded recent-window value through freshness metadata.

The reviewed evidence proves recent Riot-vs-stored comparison but does not prove missing recent matches were backfilled into LeagueBack and then surfaced through the DB-first route without duplicates.

components/dashboard/lol-stats-dashboard.tsx only reloads stored history when analyzedCount > 0, so stale-only reanalysis leaves the current visible history stale and fails to surface refreshed derived rows.
app/api/match-performance/route.ts overwrites latest_db_match_id/latest_db_match_created_at on every reanalysis, allowing older stale rows processed later to move durable latest-db metadata backward.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add Supabase migration/advisor workflow guidance, or use a dedicated database-focused skill, for features that primarily modify live schema rather than UI behavior.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: leagueback-player-sync-metadata was assigned web-feature-worker even though the implementation centered on repo migrations and live Supabase schema work, and the reviewer flagged the current skill as UI/browser-oriented.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document that returning-player stale detection must be match-specific and that stale-only refreshes must reload visible stored history while keeping latest-db metadata monotonic during stale reanalysis.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both leagueback-player-sync-metadata and returning-player-stale-derived-refresh failed because player-level metadata and stale-only refresh semantics were underspecified in shared architecture notes.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Record a stable returning-player validation account and the expected live evidence pattern for data-freshness follow-up work.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: returning-player-recent-reconciliation review noted the worker had to rediscover a suitable returning-player account and hand-build comparison evidence before validation.
